mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL levels indicate accelerating inflation expectations, which compress real discount rates and drive equity valuations higher over the near term.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of periods when CPIAUCSL increases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in UNRATE exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns fail to materialize in over 60% of triggered instances, or the signal produces a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in GDPC1 and contraction in RSAFS indicates a supply-demand imbalance where consumer spending outpaces savings, fueling corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 2-year window, or 60-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDPC1 rises and RSAFS falls.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO levels during a monetary tightening cycle reflect constrained production capacity, which suppresses corporate earnings growth and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns exceed 65% of observations during tightening periods when INDPRO declines, or the mechanism fails to separate from baseline returns in a regime-filtered backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in HOUST signals robust housing market activity, which correlates with higher consumer confidence and durable goods spending, driving positive equity market momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: The HOUST series produces a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods following housing starts increases.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS rate level exceeding 2.0% triggers a repricing of growth equity valuations, as higher borrowing costs compress future cash flow present values and force negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of periods when the federal funds rate crosses above 2.0%, or the threshold signal yields a negative information coefficient in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS combined with falling UNRATE indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer purchasing power, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid expansion in RSAFS during a post-shock recovery phase reflects pent-up consumer demand release, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and positive equity market performance over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of periods following RSAFS spikes during recovery phases, or the signal predictive power vanishes when filtered for non-recovery regimes.
